description: TP=1 enforce-eager max-num-seqs=1 — iter 0 baseline (re-verified on current flake)
drop --enforce-eager → CUDA graphs at BS=1; 2.30x over baseline
ngram spec-decoding (k=5,window=2-4) REGRESSED — likely defeats BS=1 CUDA graphs / verify pass overhead exceeds spec hit gain
kv-cache fp8: within noise of cuda-graphs (Δ -0.94%); simpler wins → discard
VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_MOE_MXFP4_MXFP8=1: MoE intermediate activations FP8 → 3.81x baseline / 1.66x iter-1
compilation pass fusions on top of mxfp8-moe: Δ -2.17% vs iter-4 (within noise but slightly negative); simpler wins
kv-cache fp8 + mxfp8-moe combo: Δ -1.25% vs iter-4 — KV bandwidth still negligible vs MoE matmul at BS=1
VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1: Δ -0.022% vs iter-4 (statistically identical); sampler off the critical path
cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1]: Δ -3.22% vs iter-4 — limiting captures didn't help, slight regression
no-chunked-prefill + no-prefix-cache + no-log-stats: Δ -2.20% vs iter-4 — scheduler overhead wasn't material; chunked-prefill disabled may force slower codepath
CUTLASS variant of MXFP4_MXFP8 MoE: Δ -32.6% vs iter-4 — TRTLLM-gen kernel is decisively faster at BS=1
max-model-len 2048: Δ -2.01% vs iter-4 + 5/50 prompts overflowed (HTTP 400) — slower AND correctness regression
VLLM_USE_OINK_OPS=1: vllm 0.19.1 + Oink incompat — TypeError unhashable list in logger.warning_once during engine init
VLLM_USE_DEEP_GEMM_E8M0=0: Δ +0.54% vs iter-4 (within noise); discard per simpler-wins-on-equal-results
sglang TP=1 trtllm_mha cuda-graph-bs=1: 343.41 tok/s — cross-engine baseline; vLLM iter-4 (371.21) still wins by 8.1%
VLLM_FLASHINFER_MOE_BACKEND=latency on top of MXFP4_MXFP8: backend selection unchanged (MXFP4_MXFP8 path overrides), Δ +0.57% noise
vLLM EAGLE3 (RedHatAI draft, k=3) on top of iter-4: Δ -33.0% — same pattern as ngram, spec invalidates BS=1 CUDA graphs in vLLM
vLLM nightly 0.20.1rc1.dev129+ga3ec4a35f + flashinfer 0.6.8.post1 + torch 2.11.0+cu130 — re-bench of iter-4 config: NEW BEST, +3.93% over iter-4
TRT-LLM 1.3.0rc13 TP=1 pytorch backend (mpirun -np 1): 388.55 — slightly above vLLM nightly best (385.80, +0.71%); cross-engine new best, 3.99x baseline
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 (NVIDIA short-context draft, k=3) + PDL: Δ -42.0% vs trt-llm baseline — third-engine confirmation that spec decoding hurts BS=1 here
sglang EAGLE3 (lmsys draft, k=3) on top of sglang baseline (343.41): Δ -25.5% — second-engine confirmation spec hurts BS=1
EAGLE3 k=3 + cuda_graph batch_sizes=[1,4] (vs iter-19's just max_batch_size=4): 222.13 — basically same as iter-19 (225.37); cuda graph capture wasn't the root cause
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 k=2 short-context: 243.15 tok/s — better than k=3 (222) but still -37.4% vs baseline (388.55)
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 k=3 with nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3 (default, NOT -short-context): 462.58 tok/s — NEW BEST, +19.05% over trt-llm baseline, 4.75x over original
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 k=3 with nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3-v2 draft: 219.81 — worse than default Eagle3 (462.58); v2 doesn't help here
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 k=3 with nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3-throughput draft: 218.64 — also regresses; only default Eagle3 draft wins
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 default draft k=2: 477.67 tok/s — NEW BEST, +3.26% over k=3 (iter-23); smaller verify wins, 4.90x baseline
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 default draft k=4: 434.70 — Δ -9.0% vs k=2; verify cost grows faster than acceptance gain past k=2
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 with RedHatAI draft: rejected — config.json missing model_type (vllm-speculator format, not trt-llm compatible)
vLLM nightly + EAGLE3 nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3 draft k=2: 474.03 tok/s — NEW BEST FOR vLLM, +22.9% over vllm nightly mxfp8; tied within noise with TRT-LLM iter-26
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 default k=1: 438.74 — Δ -8.2% vs k=2; not enough drafts to amortize verify, k=2 confirmed peak (k=1:438, 2:478, 3:462, 4:435)
sglang + nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3 draft k=2: 287.73 — Δ -16.2% vs sglang baseline; sglang's EAGLE3 path regresses regardless of draft (cf trt-llm/vllm where same draft wins)
TRT-LLM Eagle3-v2 + NVIDIA low-latency flags + k=2: 240.00 — Δ -49.8% vs iter-26; v2 draft not a fit for our workload despite vendor positioning
TRT-LLM Eagle3-v2 + LL flags + k=3: 221.01 — even worse than v2 k=2; v2 draft regresses across all k tested for our workload
TRT-LLM Eagle3-v2 + LL flags + k=3: 221.01 — even worse than v2 k=2; v2 draft regresses across all k for our workload

TRT-LLM default Eagle3 k=2 + NVIDIA full LL flags (eagle3_one_model, enable_min_latency, no autotuner, stream_interval=20, postprocess_workers=2): 466.53 — Δ -2.33% vs iter-26 — LL flags net-negative for this workload
vLLM SuffixDecoding via arctic-inference 0.1.2: incompat with vllm 0.20+ (FlexibleArgumentParser moved, EngineArgs._is_v1_supported_oracle removed). Would need arctic update or vllm 0.11 downgrade.
vLLM P-EAGLE (amazon/gpt-oss-120b-p-eagle k=5): 265.99 — Δ -43.9% vs iter-29; parallel-drafting head doesn't beat sequential nvidia draft for our workload
vLLM Recipe — EAGLE3 nvidia k=2 + custom_ops:[+rms_norm] + fuse_allreduce_rms + eliminate_noops: 477.84 — NEW BEST FOR vLLM, ties TRT-LLM iter-26
vllm EAGLE3 + per-query guided_json={type:object}: 475.32 — Δ -0.53% vs iter-38 best; loose schema doesn't extract enough determinism. Field names + enum values stay model-decided
vllm EAGLE3 + guided_json={type:object} + llguidance backend (vs xgrammar): 472.78 — Δ -1.06% vs iter-38 best; loose schema gives nothing to fast-forward on regardless of backend
vllm EAGLE3 + per-class precise schemas (intent/extractor) + llguidance: 474.47 — Δ -0.71% vs iter-38; EAGLE3 already captures the deterministic patterns, grammar fast-forward adds nothing on top
TRT-LLM Lookahead decoding: rejected — 'Speculation type Lookahead does not support backend pytorch'. Requires TRT-engine build path which isn't available for gpt-oss MXFP4.
TRT-LLM EAGLE3 + piecewise CUDA graph + userbuffers (no Inductor — Inductor crashes on custom MXFP4 kernel via FakeTensor): 421.63 — Δ -11.7% vs iter-26; piecewise alone hurts
vLLM cudagraph_mode=FULL_DECODE_ONLY (vs default FULL_AND_PIECEWISE): 460.94 — Δ -3.54% vs iter-38 best; full-decode-only graph can't cleanly capture FlashInfer TRTLLM-gen attention
vLLM use_inductor_graph_partition:true on iter-38 base: 250.72 — Δ -47.5% massive regression; partition fights with EAGLE3+MoE custom kernels

H100x2 baseline — Recipe carried from B200 iter-38 with VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_MOE_MXFP4_MXFP8 removed (SM100+ only). 197.84 tok/s — first H100x2 measurement; sits at 41.4% of B200 iter-38 (477.84). Default MXFP4 MoE backend on SM90 lands on Triton (TRTLLM_MXFP4_BF16 is SM100+, AITER is ROCm).

VLLM_MXFP4_USE_MARLIN=1 on H100x2: 320.78 tok/s — NEW H100 BEST, +62.2% over iter-46. The default Triton MoE backend on Hopper is decisively beaten by Marlin's W4A16 kernel at BS=1 (AOT-compiled, plugged into the same torch.compile + cudagraph stack). Spec acceptance also rose 42.7% → 51.1% (lower per-step latency = tighter accept window).

VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_MOE_MXFP4_BF16=1 (FLASHINFER_CUTLASS_MXFP4_BF16) — the other Hopper-reachable MXFP4 backend the oracle exposes. 196.09 tok/s — Δ -38.9% vs iter-47, basically tied with iter-46 baseline. JIT-compiles 181 sm90 CUTLASS kernels on first run (~15 min). Marlin wins decisively for BS=1 on H100.

Marlin + drop fuse_allreduce_rms (Blackwell-tuned FlashInfer fused-allreduce-norm kernel): 318.94 — Δ -0.6% vs iter-47 (noise). Pass is neutral on H100x2 NVLink TP=2 with single-token allreduces. Keep it on.

Marlin + nvidia/gpt-oss-120b-Eagle3-v2 (use_aux=False) + k=3: CRASH (IndexError: tuple index out of range in _dummy_run) THEN, after AOT compile cache invalidated, ran but acceptance was 0.2% (3/1248 drafted accepted). Two vllm 0.20.1 bugs: (1) compile cache key omits aux_hidden_state_layers — switching draft head silently reuses graph from previous Eagle3-v1 compile that returns aux tuple; (2) v2 hidden-state plumbing produces near-zero acceptance even when cache is fresh. Cannot use v2 in this nightly.

Marlin + Eagle3 (orig) k=3: 310.25 — Δ -3.3% vs iter-47 (k=2). Mean acceptance length 2.08 (vs k=2: ~2.0); per-position 0.591/0.314/0.170 — third position only 17% accept. Verify cost grows faster than acceptance gain. k=2 confirmed peak on H100x2 (mirrors B200 iter-26 finding for trt-llm).

Marlin + VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE={fp8,int8}: both CRASH. fp8 → ValueError 'Marlin W4A8-FP8 only support SM89 or SM120 device' (H100 is SM90, not eligible). int8 → RuntimeError 'MXFP4 weight + INT8 activation is not supported'. The W4A8 paths are gated to specific SMs and don't combine with MXFP4 weights anyway. Dead end on Hopper for this model.
Marlin + VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1: 317.94 — Δ -0.9% vs iter-47 (noise). Sampler off the critical path for this BS=1 + spec-decode workload, same finding as B200 iter-7 (371.13 vs 371.21).

Marlin + VLLM_USE_NCCL_SYMM_MEM=1: 318.74 — Δ -0.6% vs iter-47 (noise). NVLink + custom_all_reduce already optimal at TP=2 BS=1; symmetric-memory setup overhead doesn't amortize on single-token allreduces.

Marlin + --enforce-eager (no torch.compile, no cudagraphs): 70.79 — Δ -78% vs iter-47. The compile/cudagraph stack contributes a 4.5x multiplier on top of Marlin at BS=1 by eliminating per-launch overhead and fusing all the small ops between MoE blocks (RMSNorm, residual, projections, attention metadata). Pure calibration — never ship eager.

H100 single-GPU (TP=1) version of iter-47 winner: 246.13 tok/s. TP scaling vs iter-47 (TP=2): 320.78 / 246.13 = 1.30x for 2x GPUs — typical memory-bandwidth-bound MoE behavior at BS=1. Per-GPU is actually 53% better at TP=1 (246 vs 160 tok/s/GPU). Useful single-GPU baseline.

H100 TP=1 TRT-LLM (port of B200 iter-26 winner): 141.73 tok/s — much slower than vllm's 246 at the same platform. trt-llm 1.3.0 default MoE on Hopper falls back to TritonFusedMoE (the TRTLLM MoE backend is Blackwell-only); no Marlin equivalent exists. Cross-engine baseline at H100 TP=1.

TRT-LLM TP=1 + force moe_config.backend=CUTLASS (instead of default Triton fallback): 105.36 — Δ -25.7% vs iter-57. AutoTuner cache-misses on trtllm::fused_moe::gemm1/gemm2 → uses fallback tactic, not the autotuned kernel. CUTLASS is opt-in but worse than Triton on Hopper MXFP4 in 1.3.0. (Research agent's recommendation that "trt-llm auto-picks Hopper-native CUTLASS" was incorrect — default is Triton.)
